one_time_description: Bayside Development Corporation revenue of $3,093,238 in 2020 vs $7,833 in 2019 — likely related to new site development activity, highly anomalous
economic_notes: Bayside Development Corporation operating at Bayside site; Bayside Gas and Convenience (3318639 Nova Scotia Limited) began operating June 22, 2020 post year-end. First Nation operates Tobacco Fund, Fisheries, Gas Bar, and VLT. COVID-19 caused temporary closure of some commercial operations.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Bayside Development Corporation revenue spiked to $3,093,238 from $7,833 in prior year — highly anomalous and likely one-time. Note 13 (Government transfers) appears blank/missing from the text provided — government transfer breakdown not available, so isc_funding uses receivable balance as proxy and total_government_transfers set to 0. Restricted cash includes replacement reserve fund ($328,695) and debt reserve fund ($540,111) totalling $868,806. FNFA loan of $8,950,000 at 2.72% maturing October 2049 is a major long-term obligation. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event with temporary commercial closures. No remuneration schedule provided. num_subsidiaries estimated at 5 consolidated unincorporated entities (Tobacco Fund, Fisheries, Gas Bar, VLT, Bayside Development Corporation). expenses_housing is sum of CMHC 13537394 ($5,778) + CMHC 19042894 ($218,850) + Non-subsidized housing ($132,670) = $357,298. expenses_enterprises is sum of Tobacco Fund ($2,236,470) + Fisheries ($1,073,157) + Gas Bar ($1,112,291) + VLT ($636,313) + Bayside ($109,775) less depreciation allocated elsewhere.
